title: vittadinia triloba var. megacephala
intro: Vittadinia triloba var. megacephala, also known as the Woolly New South Wales daisy or Woolly daisy bush, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its leaves and flowers having antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Vittadinia triloba var. megacephala have been utilized to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, offering potential health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
